title: Helping with Chores
summary: A parent asks her child to help with household chores and they discuss what needs to be done.

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: Tom, can you help me with the chores today?

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Yes, Mum. What do you need me to do?

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: First, can you tidy your bedroom? It is very messy.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Okay. I will put my toys in the box and make my bed.

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: Good. After that, can you take out the rubbish?

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Sure. Where is the rubbish bag?

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: It is in the kitchen, next to the back door.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: All right. I will do that after I tidy my room.

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: Thank you. I also need to wash the dishes. Can you dry them for me?

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Of course! I am good at drying dishes.

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: Wonderful. If you help me today, we can go to the park this afternoon.

body.speaker: Child
body.text: Really? That is great! I will work very fast.

body.speaker: Parent
body.text: Ha ha! Take your time, but do it properly.

annotations.exampleEn: I do my chores every Saturday morning.
Please tidy your desk before you leave.
His bedroom is always very messy.
I make my bed every morning before school.
Can you take out the rubbish, please?
Put everything in the rubbish bag.
It is your turn to wash the dishes tonight.
Can you dry the dishes after I wash them?
I promise I will finish my homework tonight.
Take your time — there is no hurry.
The bag is next to the door.

grammarNotes.title: will for future plans and promises
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Will + verb' dùng để nói về kế hoạch hoặc lời hứa trong tương lai: 'I will put my toys in the box.' / 'I will work very fast.' / 'I promise.' Đây là cách đơn giản nhất để nói về tương lai ở cấp độ A1.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Will + verb' is used for future plans and promises: 'I will put my toys in the box.' / 'I will work very fast.' It is the simplest way to talk about the future at A1 level.

grammarNotes.title: can for requests and ability
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Can + verb' có hai nghĩa chính: khả năng ('I am good at drying dishes') và yêu cầu lịch sự ('Can you help me?'). Khi dùng để yêu cầu, 'Can you...?' là cách hỏi thân thiện và tự nhiên trong tiếng Anh hàng ngày.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Can + verb' has two main meanings: ability ('I am good at drying dishes') and polite requests ('Can you help me?'). When used for requests, 'Can you...?' is a friendly and natural form in everyday English.

translationVi: Mẹ: Tom, hôm nay con có thể giúp mẹ việc nhà không? Con: Vâng, mẹ. Mẹ cần con làm gì ạ? Mẹ: Trước tiên, con có thể dọn dẹp phòng ngủ của mình không? Nó rất bừa bãi. Con: Được ạ. Con sẽ bỏ đồ chơi vào hộp và dọn giường. Mẹ: Tốt. Sau đó, con có thể đổ rác không? Con: Được ạ. Túi rác ở đâu vậy mẹ? Mẹ: Nó ở trong bếp, bên cạnh cửa sau. Con: Được rồi. Con sẽ làm sau khi dọn phòng. Mẹ: Cảm ơn con. Mẹ cũng cần rửa bát đĩa. Con có thể lau khô cho mẹ không? Con: Tất nhiên rồi! Con giỏi lau đĩa lắm. Mẹ: Tuyệt vời. Nếu con giúp mẹ hôm nay, chiều nay chúng ta có thể đi công viên. Con: Thật à? Tuyệt quá! Con sẽ làm rất nhanh. Mẹ: Ha ha! Cứ từ từ thôi, nhưng phải làm cẩn thận nhé. Con: Vâng, mẹ. Con hứa.

questions.prompt: What does the parent ask the child to do first?
questions.hint: Find the word 'first' in the dialogue.

questions.prompt: Why does the bedroom need tidying?
questions.hint: Look at the parent's description of the bedroom.

questions.prompt: What does the child say he will put in the box?
questions.explanation: 'I will put my toys in the box.'
questions.hint: Find the child's plan for tidying.

questions.prompt: What is the second chore the parent asks the child to do?
questions.explanation: 'After that, can you take out the rubbish?'
questions.hint: Find the chore with the word 'after that'.

options: In the kitchen next to the back door
questions.explanation: 'It is in the kitchen, next to the back door.'
questions.hint: Find the parent's answer about the bag's location.

questions.prompt: What does the parent need to do with the dishes?
questions.explanation: 'I also need to wash the dishes.'
questions.hint: Find the sentence about the parent's job with the dishes.

questions.prompt: What does the parent ask the child to do with the dishes?
questions.hint: Find the parent's request about the dishes.

questions.prompt: What does the child say he is good at?
questions.hint: Find the child's statement about his skill.

questions.prompt: What reward does the parent offer?
options: Going to the park in the afternoon
questions.explanation: 'If you help me today, we can go to the park this afternoon.'
questions.hint: Find the parent's offer to the child.

questions.prompt: How does the child say he will work?
questions.hint: Find the child's reaction to the park offer.

questions.prompt: What does the parent tell the child about how to do the chores?
How does the child end the conversation?
questions.hint: Look at the child's last line.

blanks.explanation: 'Can you help me with the chores today?'
'What do you need me to do?'
'I will put my toys in the box.'
'It is in the kitchen, next to the back door.'
'I also need to wash the dishes.'
'We can go to the park this afternoon.'

criticalThinkingQuestion: The parent offers to take the child to the park if he helps with chores. Do you think giving rewards for doing chores is a good idea? What might happen if children always expect rewards for helping at home?
